_comment_top: R6 Pillar 6b / D-C-06 / task 055 — seed row for the UpdateWorkspaceTabHandler chat tool. Surfaces a single LLM-visible 'update_workspace_tab(tabId, widgetData, expectedLastUserEditAt?)' function that mutates an existing WorkspaceTab via IWorkspaceStateService.UpsertTabAsync with Q8 USER-WINS conflict resolution.
_comment_routing: Runtime invocation routes to the C# class via sprk_handlerclass = 'UpdateWorkspaceTabHandler' (= nameof handler class). Auto-discovered via ToolFrameworkExtensions.AddToolHandlersFromAssembly (ADR-010) — ZERO new top-level DI line.
_comment_placement_boundary: ADR-013 binding: the handler injects IWorkspaceStateService directly (BFF-internal workspace plumbing) — NOT through a PublicContracts facade. Mirrors task 054 (SendWorkspaceArtifactHandler) and task 053 (SprkChatAgentFactory.ResolveTools system-prompt block) — workspace state is a chat-session state primitive owned by Pillar 6a, not an AI capability.
_comment_q8_conflict_resolution: Spec FR-40 + project Q8 binding: USER WINS. The handler fetches the current tab via GetTabsAsync, compares its LastUserEditAt against the LLM-supplied expectedLastUserEditAt, and REFUSES the write when stored > expected. The refusal is a structured 'stale_read' response (success=true; data.status='stale_read'; data.currentLastUserEditAt populated) — NOT an error — so the LLM pattern-matches on data.status in its next-turn reasoning. Task 058 will wire the corresponding persona-prompt snippet instructing the agent to re-read the tab and re-attempt. No OT/CRDT in R6.
_comment_security: sprk_requiredcapability = null (intentional). Updating a workspace tab is a default user affordance available in every chat session. The handler's own gates — tab must exist + be owned by chat session's tenant + must have CanEdit=true + widgetData.kind must match existing widgetType — are the authorization surface.
_comment_tenant_isolation: ADR-014 binding: the chat session's TenantId is forwarded into IWorkspaceStateService.GetTabsAsync + UpsertTabAsync (Redis key + Cosmos partition key). Cross-tenant mutation is structurally impossible. The handler ALSO asserts current.TenantId == context.TenantId as defense in depth in case a future cache-layer bug surfaces a foreign-tenant tab.
_comment_adr015: ADR-015 data governance: telemetry logs handler name + decision (applied | refused_stale_read | refused_not_found | refused_not_editable | refused_tenant_mismatch | refused_kind_mismatch) + tabId + deterministic IDs + duration ONLY. NEVER the LLM-supplied widgetData body, NEVER the tab's existing widget content, NEVER user message text. The expectedLastUserEditAt is logged as a boolean presence flag — the actual timestamp value is governed (it reveals user-edit cadence).
_comment_pane_event_bus: ADR-030 binding: NO new SSE channel or PaneEventBus event type introduced by this handler. The client-side 'workspace.tab_edited' PaneEventBus event (task 060 additive types) is dispatched by the frontend tab manager when it observes the polled state delta — server-side responsibility ends at persistence via UpsertTabAsync. Polling channel = existing R6 Pillar 6a workspace state endpoint.
_comment_description_loadbearing: sprk_description is LOAD-BEARING per NFR-12: the LLM uses the description text to decide WHEN to invoke this tool AND how to populate the expectedLastUserEditAt argument. The phrasing makes the stale_read consequence explicit so the LLM knows to capture the timestamp when reading workspace state into its prompt (system-prompt snapshot from task 053).
_comment_consumption: Read by scripts/Seed-TypedHandlers.ps1. Idempotent — script queries by sprk_handlerclass + sprk_toolcode with sprk_name 'SYS-%' filter; UPSERTs.
sprk_description: Update an existing workspace tab's widget data. Use this when you have refined a previously dispatched artifact and the user should see the updated state on the same tab (instead of a new one). Conflict resolution: pass the 'expectedLastUserEditAt' timestamp you observed when you last read the tab from your system-prompt snapshot. If the user has edited the tab since then, this tool will REFUSE with a 'stale_read' status — you must re-read the workspace state in your next turn and re-attempt with the refreshed timestamp. User edits always win. Omit expectedLastUserEditAt only when the tab has never been user-edited (e.g., you just created it via send_workspace_artifact).
_comment_availableincontexts: 100000001 = Chat only. Workspace tab mutation is a chat-surface affordance — playbook nodes write to sprk_analysisoutput + sprk_workingdocument, not the chat-session per-tenant workspace tab list.

sprk_jsonschema.title: UpdateWorkspaceTabHandler Request
sprk_jsonschema.description: Argument shape for chat-driven invocation of the update_workspace_tab tool. Mutates an existing WorkspaceTab via IWorkspaceStateService.UpsertTabAsync with Q8 USER-WINS conflict resolution.

tabId.description: Stable workspace tab identifier (matches WorkspaceTab.id from the system-prompt snapshot).

kind.description: Discriminator — MUST equal the existing tab's widgetType. Updating a tab's widgetType is not supported; close the tab and create a new one instead.

widgetData.description: Replacement widget-data payload. Same per-variant shape as send_workspace_artifact: Summary requires {body, tldr?}; DocumentViewer requires {documentId, filename, mimeType, sizeBytes, hasSelection?, selectionText?}; Dashboard requires {layoutId, dashboardName, lastViewedSection?}; Table requires {rowCount, filteredColumns[], selectedRows[], sortColumn?, sortDirection?, dataSourceId?}. Deterministic identifiers + display metadata only — ADR-015 binding: NEVER raw user message text.

expectedLastUserEditAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the agent observed for the tab's lastUserEditAt when it last read the tab from its system-prompt snapshot. Omit (null) only when the tab has never been user-edited (e.g., the agent just created it via send_workspace_artifact). When supplied, the handler refuses the write if the current stored lastUserEditAt is later than this value (Q8: user wins).

sprk_configuration._comment: No method-discriminator dispatch — this row exposes a single LLM-facing function. Empty object reserved for future per-row defaults (e.g., per-tenant 'always-pin-after-update' policy).
